Home.title: Crabagent Console
Home.subtitle: Trace and token governance for OpenClaw.
Home.tracesHint: Open Messages for the list; detail pages use SSE (?token=) when the Collector requires an API key.

RiskOverview.title: Risk Overview
RiskOverview.kpiCommandLoopAlertsHint: Uses the same time range as Command execution (shared storage). Counts (trace, command) pairs hitting the loop threshold in the window, from shell summary.
RiskOverview.kpiCommandRedundantReadsHint: Same time range as Command execution. Read-like commands repeated ≥3 times in one trace (shell summary; not resource redundant_read).
RiskOverview.chartWorkspaceDistributionHint: Risk event count distribution by workspace
RiskOverview.chartSensitivePathGroupTrendHint: Daily trend for sensitive path access, redundant reads, credential and secret risk, and large reads
RiskOverview.chartCommandExecGroupTrendHint: Daily trend for permission denied, invalid commands, command loops, and sensitive command or token risk
RiskOverview.topHighRiskWorkspacesHint: Workspaces with the most risk events

Overview.title: Overview
Overview.subtitle: Workspace summary and key metrics will appear here.
Overview.needCollector: Configure the Collector URL in Settings first.
Overview.kpiViewTracesAria: Open traces in the message list
Overview.kpiDeepLinkMessagesAria: Open the message list with the current statistics time window
Overview.kpiDeepLinkSpansErrAria: Open execution steps with the statistics time window and error or timeout filters
Overview.kpiDeepLinkModelErrAria: Open execution steps with the statistics window, LLM span type, and error or timeout filters
Overview.kpiDeepLinkModelDurAria: Open execution steps with the statistics window and LLM span type filter
Overview.kpiDeepLinkModelTokensAria: Open execution steps with the statistics window and LLM span type filter (matches token metric scope)
Overview.kpiDeepLinkToolCallsAria: Open execution steps with the statistics window and tool span type filter
Overview.kpiDeepLinkToolErrAria: Open execution steps with the statistics window, tool span type, and error or timeout filters
Overview.kpiDeepLinkToolDurAria: Open execution steps with the statistics window and tool span type filter (matches tool duration metric scope)
Overview.sampleNote: Loaded {traces} traces and {spans} spans for charts (within paging caps).
Overview.hintUsage: Total messages in the time window.
Overview.hintSpanErr: Share of records in error or timeout status within the sample.
Overview.hintTokens: Sum of trace total_tokens; MoM compares to the previous window of equal length.
Overview.hintToolCalls: Total count of spans with span_type tool.
Overview.hintToolDur: Simple average of tool span duration_ms.
Overview.hintTokenSplit: Without prompt/completion split from the API, Input/Output use a 58%/42% split to show dual-series trends.
Overview.hintModelQps: Uses the same model filter as above, then filters traces by status. QPS = daily count ÷ 86400; QPM = daily count ÷ 1440 (rough daily average rate).
Overview.hintTokenRate: Daily token sum spread per second.
Overview.hintTtft: Proxied by average LLM span duration (not strict TTFT).
Overview.hintTpot: LLM span duration / tokens scaled to ms/token trend.
Overview.hintModelDist: LLM spans grouped by model field.
Overview.hintModelErrDist: Count of errored LLM spans; finer codes pending API.
Overview.hintToolVol: Daily sum of tool_call_count on traces.
Overview.hintToolOk: Daily success share among tool spans.
Overview.hintAgentModels: Approximate LLM calls per trace-day from spans.
Overview.hintMessages: Traces per day (same scale as volume for a second view).
Overview.hintServiceQps: QPS estimate from daily trace counts.
Overview.activityTimelineSubtitle: Estimated from session span (first / last activity). Time zone: local.
Overview.activityTimelineHint: Shows activity distribution across 24 hours to highlight peak usage.

Logs.title: Logs
Logs.subtitle: Searchable log stream and ingestion history will appear here.

Analytics.title: Analytics
Analytics.subtitle: Token waste heatmap: each column is a user turn in a conversation. Darker red means more prompt tokens (context) relative to completion and visible assistant text — typical of empty spins, tool retries, or oversized history on that turn.
Analytics.wasteHeatmapHint: Rows are recent chats (from inbound messages). Numbers are relative intensity within this sample (0–100). Hover a cell for token totals.
Analytics.wasteHeatmapEmpty: No conversations with user messages yet. Run OpenClaw with the trace plugin, then open Messages to ingest data.
Analytics.wasteLegendDash: — no LLM completion in that turn
Analytics.wasteTooltipNoLlm: No llm_output in this turn window

Machines.title: Machines
Machines.subtitle: Connected hosts and agent runtimes will appear here.

Alerts.title: Alerts
Alerts.subtitle: Notification rules and incident history will appear here.
Alerts.helpFaqBlurb: “Silence / suppression” here is local to this page for investigation noise; it is separate from persisted alert rules in the collector. Open Advanced in the editor when you need technical fields (source table, match type, and so on).
Alerts.noRulesHint: Start with a template below, or create a custom rule from scratch.
Alerts.noHistory: No events yet. After saving a rule, use “Test notify” to verify the webhook.
Alerts.formFrequencyWindowed: Aggregate events in a time window
Alerts.formFrequencyHelp: “Send immediately” uses a 1-minute window and threshold 1 for the fastest notification. “Time window” lets you set the window length and how many / how much must be exceeded.
Alerts.formImmediateNote: Immediate: saves as a 1-minute window with comparison threshold 1 to trigger a webhook as soon as possible.
Alerts.formSubWindowHint: 0 disables. When set, the outer window is split into sub-windows; the max count across sub-windows is compared to the threshold (any_max). Sub-window must be smaller than the outer window.
Alerts.testNotifyQueued: Test webhook queued. Refresh history in a few seconds to see the result.
Alerts.loadRulesError: Failed to load rules. Check collector URL, API key, and network.
Alerts.tpl_sec_enforce_hit_desc: Fires when an action hits a configured policy and is blocked, for compliance trails and triage. Condition: intercepted = 1; window: 1 min.
Alerts.tpl_resource_sensitive_path_desc: Detects access to sensitive or restricted paths to reduce overreach and data exposure. Condition: risk_flags includes sensitive_path; window: 5 min.
Alerts.tpl_resource_secret_credential_hint_desc: Flags possible keys, credentials, or high-sensitivity hints in the access path; review with priority. Condition: secret_hint or credential_hint matched; window: 5 min.
Alerts.tpl_cmd_permission_denied_burst_desc: A spike of permission-denied outcomes in the window may indicate policy, probing, or misconfiguration. Condition: permission_denied = 1 and count >= 3; window: 10 min.
Alerts.tpl_cmd_not_found_burst_desc: A cluster of “command not found” failures can signal bad environments, scanning, or missing tooling. Condition: command_not_found = 1 and count >= 5; window: 10 min.
Alerts.tpl_cmd_token_risk_burst_name: Sensitive command and token risk alert
Alerts.tpl_cmd_token_risk_burst_desc: Risky commands tied to tokens or sensitive content appear repeatedly in the window. Condition: token_risk = 1 and count >= 3; window: 15 min.
Alerts.tpl_cmd_loop_alert_desc: Detects loops or heavy repeat execution: stuck automation, misfires, or risky scripts. Condition: loop alert hit or repeat count >= 3; window: 10 min.
Alerts.tpl_resource_large_read_burst_desc: A jump in large reads over a short period can indicate bulk exfil or crawling. Condition: large_read count >= 3; window: 15 min.
Alerts.tpl_resource_redundant_read_burst_desc: The same resource is read at unusually high frequency—possible misconfiguration, thrash, or scripted abuse. Condition: redundant_read or uri_repeat_count above threshold; window: 15 min.
Alerts.investigationContextHint: Prefilled from audit context · trace={traceId} · event={eventType}
Alerts.prefillSourceCardSummary: Source: investigation workbench · trace={traceId} · span={spanId} · event={eventType}
Alerts.prefillRecommended: Recommended preset: metric={metric}, operator={operator}, threshold={threshold}, window={windowMinutes}m.
Alerts.prefillRecommendedFallback: Recommended values are missing/invalid. Fallback preset applied: metric={metric}, operator={operator}, threshold={threshold}, window={windowMinutes}m.
Alerts.mappingBody: Alert rules, delivery channels, and silence controls are consolidated on this page; legacy entry points remain under Advanced (collapsed).
Alerts.localModeBody: Rules and history are currently local to this browser. Test notify creates simulation records only.

Settings.title: Settings
Settings.subtitle: Configure how this browser connects to the Crabagent Collector. Values are stored locally in your browser only.
Settings.deletePolicyConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this policy?

DataSecurity.title: Security policy
DataSecurity.subtitle: Configure sensitive information interception policies to protect data privacy.
DataSecurity.templatesIntro: Choose a pre-built rule to start with a common sensitive data type. Templates are editable and must be refined for your country, region, and business scenario.
DataSecurity.templatesNote: These templates are starting points. Review and adjust regex, targets, and redaction mode before saving.
DataSecurity.customPoliciesHint: Create, modify, and manage interception policies. Custom rules let you cover regional formats and edge cases.
DataSecurity.searchPlaceholder: Search policy id, name, pattern or targets
DataSecurity.noPoliciesHint: Click the button above to add a policy
DataSecurity.templateCardHint: Open a template to view details in the template tab.
DataSecurity.templateSummaryMobile: Common mobile number format. Region-specific variants may require adjustment.
DataSecurity.templateSummaryNationalId: Typical identity card number pattern. Confirm local ID format before use.
DataSecurity.templateSummaryEmail: Standard email address format, suitable for user and business email detection.
DataSecurity.templateSummaryIp: IPv4 address detection. IPv6 or private address rules can be added as needed.
DataSecurity.templateSummaryPostalCode: Simple postal code matcher. Different countries use different lengths and formats.
DataSecurity.templateSummaryApiKey: Common API key/token patterns. Validate with your own key formats and prefixes.
DataSecurity.templateSummaryPassword: Credential-style fields often appear as password, secret or token assignments.
DataSecurity.templateSummaryDbConnection: Common database connection URI patterns, including MongoDB, PostgreSQL, MySQL, Redis and JDBC.
DataSecurity.templateSummarySshPrivateKey: Detects SSH private key boundaries in text to prevent key leakage.
DataSecurity.templateSummaryBankCard: Typical payment card digit patterns. Use with caution for different card formats.
DataSecurity.templateSummarySocialCredit: Common 18-character enterprise credit code format used in some jurisdictions.
DataSecurity.templateSummaryLicensePlate: Vehicle license matcher. Plate formats vary by country and region.
DataSecurity.templateModalDescription: Select one of the common sensitive data templates to bootstrap a policy. You can edit the details before saving.
DataSecurity.templateModalHint: Templates are starting points and should be refined for your region and business scenario.
DataSecurity.policyPatternInvalid: Invalid regular expression. Use syntax valid for JavaScript RegExp.
DataSecurity.policyPatternWrappedLiteralNotAllowed: The /.../g or /.../gi literal form is not supported. Enter only the expression body (without surrounding / or trailing flags).
DataSecurity.policyPatternFormatTooltip:
This system uses JavaScript RegExp syntax. The Collector and agents compile your pattern with the global g flag only.

How to enter:
• Do not wrap the pattern in /slashes/; enter the expression body only.
• Type backslashes as needed (e.g. \d for digits, \b for word boundaries; \\ for a literal backslash).
• Features follow the JS engine: character classes, groups, non-capturing (?:), quantifiers, and lookaround where the runtime supports them.
• Case-insensitive matching is not done via (?i); use explicit classes (e.g. [Pp]assword) or alternation.

Example: \b1[3-9]\d'{'9'}'\b for phone-like digit runs.
DataSecurity.policyPatternExtra: RegExp used to detect sensitive text, e.g. 1[3-9]\d'{'9'}' for phone-like digits.
DataSecurity.detectionModelNoPatternHint: Model-based detection does not use a regex pattern; model options will appear here when available.
DataSecurity.policyDispositionHint: Data mask / abort run / input guard apply action and audit by default; audit-only writes logs only.
DataSecurity.policyActionExampleDataMask: e.g. replace sensitive segments with * (138****5678), display-only impact.
DataSecurity.policyActionExampleAbortRun: e.g. stop the current agent run when this policy hits.
DataSecurity.policyActionExampleInputGuard: e.g. rewrite prompt before model input and mask sensitive segments; can affect answer accuracy.
DataSecurity.policyActionExampleAuditOnly: e.g. keep content unchanged and only write audit logs for alert replay.
DataSecurity.policyEventsEmpty: No hit events for this policy.

Steps.executionStepsHint: Click to view detailed execution step information

CommandAnalysis.title: Command execution
CommandAnalysis.blurb: Understand what the agent runs in the shell, whether failures cluster, where time and tokens go, and spot repeat loops. Data comes from tool spans (bash / terminal / params.command, etc.) in collected traces.
CommandAnalysis.needCollector: Configure the Collector URL in Settings first.
CommandAnalysis.loadError: Failed to load analysis. Check Collector and API key.
CommandAnalysis.dbSourceLine: Collector DB file "{file}" · {tools} tool span(s) · {shell} shell-rule match(es) (compare with db= in startup logs)
CommandAnalysis.emptyHintNoData: No shell-like tool spans in this range. This page defaults to All time (saved separately from the messages list). If still empty: ensure traces include tool calls, redeploy the Collector with the latest matching rules, or verify tool names/params (terminal, bash, params.command, etc.).
CommandAnalysis.hintWidenRange: The Collector database has {shellTotal} shell-like tool span(s) in total, but none match the current date range or filters. Try All time, clear channel/agent/trace filters, or confirm Settings → Collector URL points at the instance whose startup log shows the DB file that actually contains data (db=… path).
CommandAnalysis.hintRuleMismatch: This database has {tools} tool span(s) but none matched shell heuristics. Top tool names: {names}. Restart Collector after upgrading, or your agent uses tool names we do not yet map (e.g. OpenClaw uses the tool name "exec" for shell).
CommandAnalysis.hintEmptyDb: This Collector database has no tool-type spans at all — likely an empty DB, wrong CRABAGENT_DB_PATH, or ingest is going to another Collector instance.
CommandAnalysis.hintExecBackfill: Found {shell} shell-like tool span(s) but agent_exec_commands is empty ({exec} row(s)). Re-ingest with an upgraded Collector or run server backfill. Summary and audit list read from agent_exec_commands.
CommandAnalysis.summaryCapped: Summary is based on the latest {n} shell-like tool spans in range (cap for performance). Narrow the date range or filters for full coverage.
CommandAnalysis.kpiCommandsHint: Rows in the command-exec table for the current range and filters (one tool invocation = one row).
CommandAnalysis.kpiTracesHint: Distinct traces that include at least one command event above.
CommandAnalysis.kpiExecFailedHint: Rows classified as failed (success false) by exit code / stderr / stdout heuristics; excludes unknown-outcome rows.
CommandAnalysis.kpiTokenRiskTotalHint: Rows flagged as token-waste risk (e.g. large stdout). Total is over the full summary scan, not the capped token_risks list. Older collectors omit this field (shown as 0).
CommandAnalysis.sectionLoops: Loop detection (same command ≥3× in one trace)
CommandAnalysis.sectionChain: Sample think → act chain (latest trace in sample)
CommandAnalysis.sectionRedundant: Repeated read-like commands in one trace
CommandAnalysis.noLoops: No repeat loops detected in sample.
CommandAnalysis.noRedundant: No redundant read pattern (≥3×) in sample.
CommandAnalysis.noTokenRisk: No stdout-heavy rows flagged in sample.
CommandAnalysis.tokenNote: Token and $ estimates: chars÷4 → tokens, × $0.50 / 1M (illustrative only).
CommandAnalysis.chartTokenRiskBuckets: Token risk by stdout size band
CommandAnalysis.chartTokenRiskBars: Token waste risk — top stdout volume
CommandAnalysis.focusTraceDetails: Filter to this trace in details
CommandAnalysis.openMessageInspectFailed: Could not load the message for this trace; it may be outside the collector search window.
CommandAnalysis.spanStepOpenMessageInspectAria: Open message details on this page and focus this execution step

RiskCenter.subtitle: Focus on high-priority risk discovery and triage decisions with concise KPI, queue, and impact modules.
RiskCenter.heroTitle: Decide what deserves attention first, then dive into details.
RiskCenter.heroBody: There are {total} risk items in the current scope, including {urgentCount} unsilenced high-priority items. The dominant signal source is {source}. Start with urgent unsilenced items, then review silence rules that are about to expire.
RiskCenter.heroChecklistUrgentBody: {count} unsilenced P0 / P1 items still need a verdict. Open the investigation workbench before tuning rules.
RiskCenter.heroChecklistUrgentBodyClear: No unsilenced P0 / P1 items remain. You can shift to rule quality and impact review.
RiskCenter.heroChecklistFocusBody: Start from {severity} risk "{summary}" so the team stays aligned on one concrete thread.
RiskCenter.heroChecklistFocusBodyClear: No clear lead risk is available right now. Use distribution and impact views to decide the next angle.
RiskCenter.heroChecklistSilenceBody: {activeCount} silence rules are active, and {expiringSoonCount} of them will expire in the next 30 minutes.
RiskCenter.severityGuideP0: Sensitive path access, critical command failure, policy interception
RiskCenter.severityGuideP1: High token risk, PII/credential hints, policy hit
RiskCenter.severityGuideP2: Large read, redundant read, command failure
RiskCenter.focusHotTraceBody: Trace {traceId} already aggregates {count} risk items, with highest severity {severity}.
RiskCenter.focusSilenceHealthBody: {activeCount} silence rules are active, and {expiringSoonCount} expire within 30 minutes.
RiskCenter.compositionSummaryBody: {source} is currently driving the largest share of the risk pool with {count} items. Review its signal quality and linked workflows first.
RiskCenter.compositionSummaryEmpty: No source distribution is available in the current scope.
RiskCenter.suggestionUrgentBody: {count} unsilenced P0 / P1 items remain. Capture verdicts before widening alert coverage.
RiskCenter.suggestionPolicyBody: {count} policy-hit items are present. Cross-check content audit and rule configs for noise or missed enforcement.
RiskCenter.suggestionSilenceBody: {count} silence rules expire within 30 minutes. Decide early whether to extend or remove them.
RiskCenter.suggestionSteadyBody: No obvious urgent items or expiring silences are present. This is a good window to improve impact coverage and rule quality.
RiskCenter.mappingBody: Legacy resource-audit capabilities are migrated into this page's priority queue and impact panel; full details remain available via Advanced.
RiskCenter.emptyQueue: No risk items in the current time range
RiskCenter.emptyQueueHint: Try broadening filters or expanding the date range
RiskCenter.emptyQueueForTrace: No risk items were found for this trace in the current range
RiskCenter.emptyQueueForTraceHint: Clear trace filter to view all risks
RiskCenter.loadErrorBody: Collector may be unreachable or temporary errors occurred. Retry after checking the connection.

Events.investigationGuideStep1: View timeline to locate suspicious events
Events.investigationGuideStep2: Filter and narrow scope by event type, source, and keyword
Events.investigationGuideStep3: Use pagination and linkage to continue cross-page investigation
Events.emptyTimeline: No investigation events in this range.
Events.emptyTimelineHint: Try broadening the date range or filters to see more data
Events.emptyTimelineForTrace: No investigation events found for this trace in the current range.
Events.loadErrorBody: Collector may be unreachable or temporary errors occurred. Retry after checking the connection.

ResourceAudit.title: Resource access audit
ResourceAudit.subtitle: Make file touches, memory/RAG retrieval, and other tool I/O explicit: who accessed what, when, in which mode, and at what volume.
ResourceAudit.positioningLine: Use this view for data-governance and ops workflows: spot sensitive-path access, unusually large reads, and repetitive access within a single message.
ResourceAudit.bulletAggregate: Aggregate by resource URI, workspace, and tool (span name). Prefix filters and full-text search help zoom from row-level detail to path-level review.
ResourceAudit.bulletRiskHeuristics: Risk tags use heuristics on paths, keywords in outputs/snippets, and read volume. They help triage and prioritization—not a substitute for DLP or formal compliance findings.
ResourceAudit.bulletSecurityAudit: For regex policy hits at ingest (policy names and counts, no plaintext), cross-check the Content audit page.
ResourceAudit.methodologySummary: Where the data comes from—and what it can’t show
ResourceAudit.methodologyBody:
Events are derived from tool spans in collected traces. The OpenClaw trace plugin enriches metadata.resource (URI, access_mode, chars, snippet, …) before ingest; the Collector also normalizes common path fields from input.

Limits: tool calls without resource metadata won’t appear here. “Memory / RAG” relies on semantic_kind. Snippets are samples and may be incomplete.
ResourceAudit.needCollector: Configure the Collector URL in Settings first.
ResourceAudit.resourceAuditTabAria: Switch between metrics and audit log
ResourceAudit.searchPlaceholder: Search URI, trace id, span name…
ResourceAudit.kpiTotalEventsHint: Total number of resource access events in the current time range.
ResourceAudit.kpiTotalEventsMergedHint: Left value is total resource events, right value is distinct messages (deduped by trace_id).
ResourceAudit.kpiDistinctTracesHint: Count of distinct trace_ids with at least one access-audit event—roughly how many user messages appear in access audit.
ResourceAudit.kpiSumCharsHint: Sum of resource.chars in current range, useful for volume tracking.
ResourceAudit.kpiRiskAnyHint: Rows where at least one of: sensitive path, policy-tag hit (pii/secret/etc.), or read size above threshold.
ResourceAudit.kpiSensitivePathHint: Number of events tagged with sensitive_path in the current range.
ResourceAudit.kpiSensitiveInfoHint: Number of events tagged with pii_hint in the current range.
ResourceAudit.kpiLargeReadHint: Number of events tagged with large_read in the current range.
ResourceAudit.kpiSecretHintHint: Number of events tagged with secret_hint in the current range.
ResourceAudit.kpiPiiHintHint: Number of events tagged with pii_hint in the current range.
ResourceAudit.kpiCredentialHintHint: Number of events tagged with credential_hint in the current range.
ResourceAudit.emptyStateTitle: No access-audit events in this range
ResourceAudit.emptyStateBody: The Collector is reachable, but no access-audit events matched your time range and filters.
ResourceAudit.emptyStateTraceBody: This trace is filtered, but it has no access-audit rows to show.
ResourceAudit.emptyChecklistPlugin: Verify the OpenClaw trace plugin is enabled and tool spans carry resource / semantic_kind metadata.
ResourceAudit.emptyChecklistRange: Widen the date range or try “All time”.
ResourceAudit.emptyChecklistFilters: Clear URI prefix, semantic-class filter, and search text.
ResourceAudit.emptyChecklistDb: Confirm the db= path in the Collector startup log points at the instance receiving traffic.
ResourceAudit.emptyRiskChart: No sensitive path / secret keyword / large read in this range.
ResourceAudit.tableIntro: Sorted by time (newest first). Hover dotted column headers for definitions. Use Links to open content audit or constrain to one message.
ResourceAudit.colBytesWrittenHint: metadata.resource.bytes_written: bytes reported by the tool or parsed from success text.
ResourceAudit.colCharsUtf8Hint: metadata.resource.chars_utf8: UTF-8 byte length of payload or read-back body text.
ResourceAudit.colCharsJsHint: metadata.resource.chars_js: JavaScript string length (UTF-16 code units) of the same body.
ResourceAudit.colTimeHint: Event time: span start_time_ms when present, otherwise the trace created_at_ms.
ResourceAudit.colExecTypeHint: Span name (span_name): tool or step identifier in the trace (e.g. read, memory_search).
ResourceAudit.colAgentHint: Agent name that triggered the resource access event (when reported by ingestion).
ResourceAudit.colChannelHint: Source channel of the event (when reported by ingestion, e.g. web / lark / telegram).
ResourceAudit.colWorkspaceHint: Opik / OpenClaw workspace—useful to separate tenants or environments.
ResourceAudit.colUriHint: Normalized resource id: prefers metadata.resource.uri, then common input.params path fields.
ResourceAudit.colClassHint: File / memory come from semantic_kind; other tool spans are grouped as tool (other).
ResourceAudit.colModeHint: access_mode from the plugin (e.g. read, write, search)—semantics depend on the tool.
ResourceAudit.colCharsHint: metadata.resource.chars: Unicode code point count (UTF-8 normalized) written by the trace plugin for read/write resource audit; empty when not set.
ResourceAudit.colDurationHint: Span duration in milliseconds—useful for slow IO or blocking tools.
ResourceAudit.colTraceHint: Opens the same message inspect view as the Spans list: semantic tree, pipeline, and I/O, focused on this step.
ResourceAudit.openMessageInspectFailed: Could not load the message for this trace; it may be outside the collector search window.
ResourceAudit.colLinkageHint: Filter this page to one trace, or open content audit with trace/span context.
ResourceAudit.colFlagsHint: Heuristic risk tags; multiple can apply. Compare with the risk breakdown chart above.

SecurityAudit.title: Content audit
SecurityAudit.sourceFootnote: Tool or plugin attribution lives on the trace span metadata.
SecurityAudit.needCollector: Configure the Collector URL in Settings first.
SecurityAudit.empty: No content audit events in this range.

Traces.title: Messages
Traces.subtitle: Each row is one message (OpenClaw / Opik). Open a session to inspect execution steps, tokens, and metadata.
Traces.searchSpansPlaceholder: Search execution step id, message id, name, session…
Traces.openclawRoutingLabelOptionalHint: No session label set in OpenClaw (same as control UI placeholder).
Traces.openclawRoutingFieldThinkingHeaderHint: How deep the model thinks (latency and cost).
Traces.openclawRoutingFieldFastHeaderHint: When on, faster replies; may trade off complex reasoning quality.
Traces.openclawRoutingFieldReasoningHeaderHint: Direct answers vs explicit reasoning—useful for technical or analytical tasks.
Traces.openclawRouteFast_onHint: Faster responses; may reduce complex reasoning quality slightly.
Traces.tableSelectAllPage: Select all messages on this page
Traces.spanTypeColumnFilterAria: Filter list by execution step type
Traces.filterDialogDescription: Filter by channel, agent, and status. Changes apply when you click Apply.
Traces.filterDialogDescriptionTraces: Filter by status. For channel and agent, use the funnel icons in the table column headers.
Traces.filterDialogDescriptionThreads: Use the funnel icons next to Agent and Channel in the table headers. You can reset all list filters below or clear selections in those menus.
Traces.filterDialogDescriptionSpans: Filter by status here or via the funnel in the table. For channel and agent, use the column header funnels.
Traces.threadsCostHint: Estimated cost aggregated from messages in this session
Traces.threadDrawerLinkHint: Event timeline for this session (types, payloads, and pipeline steps).
Traces.threadDrawerPipelineTypeCount: {count} distinct event type(s) — expand the timeline for JSON.
Traces.threadDrawerMessageTurnCount: {count} turns — select one to view the chat slice.
Traces.threadDrawerLinkEmpty: No message events for this session.
Traces.threadDrawerNoMessages: No messages loaded for this session.
Traces.threadDrawerCallGraphTruncated: Showing the first {max} traces only (session truncated).
Traces.threadDrawerCallGraphEmpty: No traces in scope for this session graph.
Traces.traceGraphClickHint: Click a node to open the trace detail.
Traces.archPipelineLegend: Horizontal pipeline aligned with the OpenClaw architecture diagram (aggregated from this trace). Amber dashed edge: tool→LLM loop when multiple LLM rounds are detected.
Traces.archPipelineClickHint: Click the LLM API stage to open the matching execution step when an LLM span exists.
Traces.archPipelineRunnerBullets: system prompt: tools · skills · memory · history & compaction
Traces.archPipelineLoopHint: Multi-turn: tool results feed the model again
Traces.executionGraphLegend: Edges: slate = parent span; amber dashed = LLM→memory; green dashed = LLM→tool (parallel); orange solid = LLM→tool (sequential); purple dashed = cross-trace; pink dashed = trace-only lineage.
Traces.executionGraphMergedLegend: Top: execution pipeline (inbound → gateway → runner → LLM → tools → response, aggregated for the primary trace). Bottom: actual span/trace edges. Gray static dashed lines map framework stages to concrete nodes. Other edges use an animated dash along the path to show data/control flow direction. LLM→tool edges are labeled Parallel/Sequential (from trace metadata tool_execution_mode). Edge colors distinguish relationship types.
Traces.executionGraphMergedClickHint: Click the LLM API stage or any span to open the execution step; click a trace header node to open that message.
Traces.executionGraphClickHint: Click a trace header to open that message; click a span node to open the same execution-step view as the list.
Traces.executionGraphSpanNotFound: Could not load this span for the current trace.
Traces.threadDrawerTurnWaitingPipelineHint: This span covers ingress, scheduling, and context prep. Without ingested session_start / before_* events, finer steps are not available.
Traces.threadDrawerTurnWaitingPipelineFootnote: Sub-steps are estimated from event timestamps and may differ slightly from actual execution order.
Traces.threadDrawerPipelineNodeNoTimestamp: No start/end time for this node
Traces.threadDrawerPipelineBlurbSessionStart: Session starts: sampling and session-level prep.
Traces.threadDrawerPipelineBlurbBeforeModelReso: Resolve model and capabilities; prep for prompt build.
Traces.threadDrawerPipelineBlurbBeforePromptBui: Build prompt and message list from history and policy.
Traces.threadDrawerPipelineBlurbBeforeAgentStar: Prep immediately before the agent run (before_* hooks).
Traces.threadDrawerPipelineBlurbHookContributio: A plugin injects content into context (see payload for source).
Traces.threadDrawerPipelineBlurbContextPrune: Context pruning applied to limit size.
Traces.threadDrawerPipelineBlurbModelStreamCont: Streaming context: model dimension, message indices, etc.
Traces.threadDrawerPipelineBlurbLlmOutput: Model reply: assistant text and usage.
Traces.threadDrawerPipelineBlurbBeforeToolCall: About to run a tool (name below).
Traces.threadDrawerPipelineBlurbAgentEnd: Turn teardown: message snapshot, success/error, duration.
Traces.threadDrawerPipelineBlurbSubagentSpawned: A subagent session or task was spawned.
Traces.threadDrawerOpenclawPipelineFootnote: Allowlisted event types only. Duration prefers event duration, then end−start, then the gap to the next node. Multiple tool rounds in one user turn produce multiple model input/output pairs; round index and provider/model come from each event payload (same fields as the trace timeline summary).
Traces.threadDrawerTurnTokensUnsplit: No prompt/completion split from provider; total ≈ {count} tokens
Traces.threadDrawerTokenPopoverCalcHint: Calculation: based on each model output within the merged scope of this left-hand message item.
Traces.threadDrawerTokenPopoverSubagentNote: Note: tokens used in a separate subagent session are not included; check that session in the subagent drawer.
Traces.tableSelectAllThreadsPage: Select all sessions on this page
Traces.threadsEmptyBody: Sessions appear after the OpenClaw trace plugin ingests batches that include thread rows.
Traces.threadsEmptyFiltered: No sessions match the current search or time range.
Traces.spanInspectAuditSelectSpanHint: Select a span in the tree on the left to see access-audit and content-audit hit summaries.
Traces.spanInspectNoResourceAudit: No access-audit row for this span (may not be a file/tool access).
Traces.spanInspectNoSecurityHits: No content-audit hits for this span.
Traces.spanInspectOpenResourceAudit: Open access audit for this trace →
Traces.spanInspectOpenSecurityAudit: Open content audit for this trace/span →
Traces.spansEmptyBody: Execution steps appear after messages are ingested with span payloads from the plugin.
Traces.spansEmptyFiltered: No execution steps match the current search or time range.
Traces.tableSubtitle: One row per message_received from the trace plugin. Agent/channel may be backfilled from earlier events on the same thread_key. Search matches message text, titles, agent fields, and ids (including message id).
Traces.empty: No events yet. Run OpenClaw with the plugin enabled.
Traces.shadowAuditHint: Observe mode: sensitive matches detected but not rewritten for the model in this turn.
Traces.autoPullAria: Automatically refetch the list from Collector about every 12 seconds
Traces.stats: {traces} session(s). Each row is the latest activity in that session.
Traces.listMessagesEmpty: No inbound messages yet. This list only includes message_received events from the trace plugin.
Traces.listTraceRecordsEmpty: No messages yet. Rows appear after the gateway ingests events with trace_root_id (trace plugin). Older data without ingest will not show here.
Traces.listTraceRecordsEmptyFiltered: No messages match the current filters. Expand the date range to “All”, clear search, or remove advanced filters.
Traces.searchPlaceholder: Message text, session key, trace_id, agent…
Traces.columnOptimizationHint: Estimated share of tokens avoided by pruning (context_prune_applied), vs spent + saved.
Traces.toolsHint: TOOL execution-step count (before_tool / after_tool, non-memory file ops)
Traces.needCollectorUrl: Set the Collector URL and API key in Settings to load messages.
Traces.probeHint: If counts are zero: ensure Collector is running, URL/API key match, and the gateway plugin is ingesting.
Traces.threadFetchCountTitle: Total rows fetched for this conversation (used for the message list). The right panel only shows the selected message’s slice.
Traces.convThinkingToggleAria: Show request snapshot behind this reply
Traces.convEmptyAssistant: No assistant reply (llm_output) in this slice yet — the run may still be in progress.
Traces.convNoTurn: Select a message to see the conversation view.
Traces.semanticDecisionNodeTitle: Branch: model turned to a tool / IO / memory execution step
Traces.detailRightPanelSubtitle: Only the processing chain for the selected message (same trace_root slice). Other messages in this chat are not shown here.
Traces.confirmDeleteSession: Delete all events for this session? This cannot be undone.
Traces.detailPageHint: Pick a user message to inspect its pipeline slice, semantic execution steps, and raw timeline. The left list loads this conversation’s events; when the Collector is reachable, live updates use SSE.
Traces.detailEmpty: No events for this conversation yet.
Traces.treeHint: Within this message’s slice only: grouped by run id. Rows without run_id are listed first in their group; then chronological order. Green badges = pipeline step. Full chain: user message → model resolve → prompt build → plugin hooks → optional prune → stream context → llm_input / llm_output (and tools per round).
Traces.pipelineModelStreamContext: Cache / stream context snapshot: seq {seq}, ~{msgs} messages, provider {provider}, model {model}. See payload for digests.
Traces.pipelineBeforeModelResolve: Early phase: user prompt {chars} chars. Provider/model are resolved here (no session transcript yet).
Traces.pipelineBeforePromptBuild: Pre-LLM assembly: {history} session messages, user prompt {promptChars} chars. Role mix: {roles}. Plugins (e.g. memory) may still prepend before llm_input.
Traces.pipelineLlmInput: Final model request: {provider} / {model}. History {history} msgs, {images} images. Approx. {delta} chars added after post-bootstrap prompt (plugin prependContext, etc.).
Traces.pipelineHookContribution: Per-plugin mutation · source hook {source} · contributing package {plugin}
Traces.pipelineContextPrune: Context prune ({mode}): messages {before}→{after}, ~chars {cBefore}→{cAfter} (Δ{delta}). Role histograms remain in payload.
Traces.pipelineContextPruneAggregateHint: Aggregate-only event: no per-message rows in payload. If your OpenClaw build adds messageChanges on context_prune_applied, the table appears automatically.
Traces.pipelineContextPruneChangesTruncated: List truncated: more transcript indices changed than rows shown. Inspect full payload below.
Traces.sessionListHint: Grouped by session key or id. Pick one to inspect runs and payloads.
Traces.deleteSessionUnavailable: This bucket has no session_id; server delete is unavailable. Events may still use only session_key.
Traces.userMessagesHint: Each row is one user message. The block under the preview is that message’s observability slice. The right column always shows only the selected message’s full processing chain — not the whole chat.
Traces.sidebarTraceNoTypesYet: Only the inbound row so far — open after the agent runs.
Traces.noTraceRootOnTurn: This turn has no trace_root_id on the event; pipeline events cannot be grouped.
Traces.noLinkedRunDetail: No following llm_input in this message view (or session mismatch). Expand after the agent runs.
Traces.detailMergedOnlyMessagesHint: This session currently has only message_received events in the database — no hooks or LLM rows yet. Usually the agent has not finished a turn, the trace plugin is off, sampling dropped events, or ingest failed. After upgrading Collector, reopen this page: split buckets (session_key vs session_id) are merged when loading events.
Traces.detailPipelineTypeCount: {count} distinct type(s) below — expand the timeline for full JSON payloads.
Traces.noRunEventsYet: No agent run events are linked to this message yet.
Traces.detailTreeNoMatches: No execution steps match the filter.
Traces.detailNoTokenStats: No token usage in this span payload.
Traces.detailFeedbackPlaceholder: Feedback and annotations are not wired yet.
Traces.semanticTreeHint: Nested by Collector `parent_id`: Agent loop → LLM → tools / IO / memory. Click a node to inspect payloads, tokens, and context diff.
Traces.semanticTreeEmpty: No execution steps in this slice.
Traces.semanticTreeEmptyDetail: No rows in `spans` for this message id yet (run a full agent turn with observability ingest). Use the raw event timeline below.
Traces.semanticTreeNeedTraceRoot: This message has no trace_root_id — semantic tree needs a linked agent run.
Traces.inspectorEmpty: Select a node on the left to see input/output, context diff, and token metrics.
Traces.inspectorToolPayloadHint: Tool returned ~{kb} KB — consider preprocessing or summarizing before the model sees it.
Traces.inspectorContextDiffHint: Line-based diff: lines only in “before” (left) are treated as removed after pruning / assembly. Reordered lines may be mislabeled.
Traces.inspectorPromptCompareHint: Word-level diff: red strikethrough = removed on the left, green = added on the right.
Traces.inspectorPromptCompareTooLarge: Content is too long for word-level diff; using line-level comparison (switch below if needed).
Traces.noTurnsExtracted: No user messages or llm_input rows found in this message view.
